form_requirement.bgb_ref: R2K §§ 1-26 (Restatement (Second) of Contracts) — persuasive; binding law is state common law plus UCC Art. 2 for goods
form_requirement.notes: US default is no form requirement. Specific carve-outs in each state's Statute of Frauds (modeled on R2K § 110) require a signed writing for certain categories: contracts not performable within one year, sale-of-land contracts, suretyship promises, marriage contracts, executor promises, and UCC § 2-201 sale-of-goods contracts for $500 or more. ESIGN Act (15 USC §§ 7001-7031) and UETA (in 49 states; NY uses ESRA) make electronic signatures and records satisfy any Statute of Frauds writing requirement.

optional_clauses.name: Offer
optional_clauses.notes: Manifestation of willingness to enter into a bargain so made as to justify another in understanding that assent will conclude it. Distinguished from preliminary negotiations and advertisements (R2K § 26).

optional_clauses.name: Acceptance
optional_clauses.notes: Manifestation of assent to the terms of the offer in the manner invited or required. Mirror-image rule under common law; relaxed by UCC § 2-207 for sale-of-goods contracts.

optional_clauses.name: Consideration
optional_clauses.notes: Bargained-for exchange of legal value. Nominal consideration generally insufficient outside option contracts (R2K § 87) and modifications of land-sale contracts. Hamer v. Sidway 124 N.Y. 538 (1891) is canonical.

optional_clauses.name: Capacity
optional_clauses.notes: Minors (under 18 generally), persons under guardianship, and persons mentally incompetent at time of contracting lack full capacity. Minor contracts voidable at minor's option, subject to necessaries exception.

optional_clauses.name: Legality of Purpose
optional_clauses.notes: Contracts unenforceable on public-policy grounds — gambling debts where prohibited, agreements in restraint of trade exceeding reasonableness, contracts inducing crime or tort, certain exculpatory clauses for gross negligence.

optional_clauses.name: Statute of Frauds Compliance
optional_clauses.notes: Six common-law categories require signed writing: contracts in consideration of marriage; contracts not performable within one year; sale-of-land contracts; executor promises; suretyship promises (the 'main purpose' exception narrows this); and under UCC § 2-201, sale of goods for $500 or more. Merchant memo exception, part performance, and admissions in pleadings cure missing writing.

optional_clauses.name: Parol Evidence Rule
optional_clauses.notes: Bars extrinsic evidence of prior or contemporaneous agreements that contradict a fully integrated written contract. Merger / integration clauses raise presumption of full integration. Williston (four-corners) vs. Corbin (intent) approaches split state by state.

optional_clauses.name: Choice of Law
optional_clauses.notes: Party-chosen law generally enforced if (1) the chosen state has a substantial relationship to the parties or transaction or there is another reasonable basis for the choice, and (2) application would not be contrary to a fundamental public policy of a state with materially greater interest. State variations apply.

optional_clauses.name: Choice of Forum / Forum-Selection
optional_clauses.bgb_ref: Atlantic Marine Constr. Co. v. U.S. Dist. Court, 571 U.S. 49 (2013)
optional_clauses.notes: Forum-selection clauses are presumptively enforceable in federal court under 28 USC § 1404(a); transfer is denied only in extraordinary circumstances. State enforcement varies — most follow Bremen v. Zapata 407 U.S. 1 (1972).

optional_clauses.name: Electronic Signature Compliance
optional_clauses.bgb_ref: 15 USC §§ 7001-7031 (ESIGN); UETA § 7 (in 49 states); NY CPLR § 304-a / ESRA
optional_clauses.notes: Electronic signature satisfies any state or federal writing requirement subject to consumer-consent rules under 15 USC § 7001(c). Carve-outs in 15 USC § 7003: wills, trusts, family-law documents, court orders, certain UCC documents (excluding sale-of-goods records), notices of cancellation of utilities, foreclosure, eviction, life/health insurance, product recalls.

court_cases.relevance: Objective theory of mutual assent — outward manifestations control over subjective intent; a 'joke' contract written on a restaurant check was enforceable where reasonable person would read the words as serious offer and acceptance. 196 Va. 493.

court_cases.case: Atlantic Marine Constr. Co. v. U.S. Dist. Court
court_cases.relevance: Forum-selection clauses are presumptively enforceable; a court considering transfer under 28 USC § 1404(a) where a valid forum-selection clause exists may not weigh the parties' private interests, only public-interest factors. 571 U.S. 49.

FAA preempts state laws (such as California's Discover Bank rule) that condition arbitration clause enforceability on the availability of class-wide proceedings; class-action waivers in consumer arbitration clauses are enforceable. 563 U.S. 333.
Establishes procedural and substantive unconscionability framework for refusing enforcement of one-sided consumer contracts. 350 F.2d 445 (D.C. Cir.).
